- generateSystemPrompt, generateMagicContent, generateMagicSuggestions
- generateSuggestions, review agent delegation, extractReceiptInfo
- summarizeFile, synthesizeDocuments, extractTextFromImage

refactor: implement PDF scrollbar as custom gpui Element

Replace composed div-based scrollbar with a proper gpui Element
implementation, following Zed's approach. The custom Element uses
request_layout/prepaint/paint phases to get actual rendered bounds,
ensuring the scrollbar fits exactly within the PDF view container
without extending under the mode line.

Key changes:
- New src/pdf/scrollbar.rs with PdfScrollbar Element implementation
- Absolute positioning with inset top/left 0 to pin to viewport
- Track + thumb rendering with paint_quad
- Hitbox-based mouse interaction (drag, click-to-jump)
- Drag state stored on PdfState entity (survives across frames)
- Removed all scrollbar/drag code from PdfView

Co-authored-by: jaypatrick <1800595+jaypatrick@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: use withInMemoryScrolling instead of withRouterConfig for scroll options

withRouterConfig() accepts RouterConfigOptions which does not include
scrollPositionRestoration/anchorScrolling/scrollOffset — those belong
in withInMemoryScrolling() (InMemoryScrollingOptions). Replace
withRouterConfig with withInMemoryScrolling to fix TS2353 compile error.

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/jaypatrick/adblock-compiler/sessions/19ba5eac-2925-47e4-84bc-193021bf2250

Co-authored-by: jaypatrick <1800595+jaypatrick@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: apply review feedback — scrollOffset, queryParamMap.get for URL guard

- app.config.ts: add scrollOffset: [0, 72] to withInMemoryScrolling so
  the 72px header offset comment matches actual behaviour
- compiler.component.ts: use queryParamMap.get('url') instead of
  queryParams['url'] at both navigate guards — queryParams['url'] can be
  string|string[], making the equality check unreliable when query params
  are repeated; queryParamMap.get() always returns string|null

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/jaypatrick/adblock-compiler/sessions/7666d75e-7e5f-45e1-be1e-eeac3388e999

Co-authored-by: jaypatrick <1800595+jaypatrick@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: move scrollOffset to withRouterConfig — not valid in InMemoryScrollingOptions

scrollOffset is a RouterConfigOptions property, not InMemoryScrollingOptions.
Moving it to withRouterConfig({ scrollOffset: [0, 72] }) fixes the
TS2353 build error introduced when applying the reviewer's suggestion.

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/jaypatrick/adblock-compiler/sessions/73661368-bb68-40d8-a383-37a220cddc87

Co-authored-by: jaypatrick <1800595+jaypatrick@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: implement scrollOffset via ViewportScroller.setOffset — not a router type option

scrollOffset is not a property of InMemoryScrollingOptions or RouterConfigOptions
in Angular 21. The correct API is ViewportScroller.setOffset([0, 72]), called inside
provideAppInitializer before any await so the injection context is still active.

- Remove withRouterConfig (not needed)
- Add ViewportScroller to @angular/common import
- Call inject(ViewportScroller).setOffset([0, 72]) in provideAppInitializer
- Update comment to describe actual behavior

Renderer: enforce 2-tile scroll margin at visible viewport edges

continue scrolling with no surrounding context. Add SCROLL_MARGIN = 2:

each visible edge, so surrounding walls/tiles are always shown.

commit.message:
Fix mobile: keep player visible when viewport clamps at map edge

Cover-mode CSS scaling clips the canvas on one axis. On portrait mobile
the canvas overflows horizontally, so only the centre ~29 of 60 columns
are visible. When the player walked to a map edge, the map-bounds clamp
(vpX = 0) pushed the player into the hidden left portion of the canvas.

- Renderer: track visibleCols/visibleRows (set via setVisibleViewport)
- Renderer.draw: two-step viewport calc — clamp to map bounds first,
  then shift to guarantee player stays within the visible CSS window
  (may render void tiles at the far edge rather than losing the player)
- Game: expose setRendererViewport() to forward values to the renderer
- main.ts: fitCanvas now returns visibleCols/visibleRows (computed from
  availW/availH divided by tile size × scale) and wires them to game

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: load standard MM3 parameters from .fld files

Add include_standard parameter to _mm3_import_ff, load_mm3_fld,
and ForceField.from_mm3_fld. When enabled, parses the full MM3
parameter body (rows 60-1800) in addition to the substructure
section, matching how MacroModel resolves parameters.

This fixes issue #197: the published Donoghue 2008 force field
was missing 30/38 bond and 55/78 angle parameters, causing
C-H stretches at 25,000 cm⁻¹ (should be <4,000). With standard
parameters loaded, the objective score drops from 139,911 to 9,530
(93% reduction).

The published FF still does not beat the Seminario baseline (~104)
due to MM3* functional form differences (stretch-bend coupling,
etc.) not implemented in OpenMM. Xfail gates remain in place.

Closes #197

commit.message:
Meeting Planner V3: slider, AM/PM, periods, always visible

Major UX overhaul based on user feedback:

1. Step 2 always visible (not hidden until 2+ cities)
2. Time slider (0-23:30) — drag to explore meeting times,
   shows each city's local time with morning/day/evening/night
   color-coded cards as you drag
3. Timeline uses morning 🌅/day ☀️/evening 🌆/night 🌙 periods
   instead of just business/extended/off — more realistic for
   global teams where 'perfect overlap' rarely exists
4. AM/PM labels on timeline (12a, 3a, 6a, 9a, 12p, 3p, 6p, 9p)
5. Hover tooltips show '3 PM in London — ☀️ Daytime'
6. Calendar date picker styled for dark theme

commit.message:
Simplify to 2 pipelines: Build + Release

Merged BuildTest into Build pipeline:
  - Runs on push to main AND on PRs
  - Build stage: builds images, injects bugs via failure_scenario
  - Test stage: validates each service (Python tests, .NET build, etc.)

Removed redundant pipelines:
  - Deleted PowerGrid-Deploy (combined, replaced by Build+Release)
  - Deleted PowerGrid-BuildTest (merged into Build)
  - Removed old azure-pipelines.yml and azure-pipelines-ci.yml

Final: 2 pipelines in ADO
  PowerGrid-Build (ID: 4) — CI: build + test
  PowerGrid-Release (ID: 5) — CD: deploy + validate

GitHub.Copilot.SDK, so types from the parent namespace resolve
automatically without an explicit using directive.

commit.message:
fix: treat disconnect during game as forfeit

When a player disconnects while actively playing, mark them as
forfeited and trigger endgame logic (emitEndGameToPlayers). This
ensures the remaining player wins if they are the last one standing,
rather than the game hanging with a disconnected ghost player.
